</thought>
<action>

The agent is at the bottom facing upward. The cell directly in front is safe floor, and there is a vertical corridor of safe cells ahead. To the left are lava cells, and to the right is a wall, so the safest progress is to continue forward through the corridor.

<thought>
The agent is facing upward, with the cell directly in front being floor, while the left side is lava and the right side is blocked by a wall. Moving forward is the only safe progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center facing upward. The cell directly in front is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to the right is a wall, so turning right would not help immediately. The open corridor extends to the left side, so I should turn left to face the safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, while the row further ahead contains lava across most of the corridor. Turning does not reveal an immediate better option, and moving forward advances safely into the open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
